item.title: BarstewardsThe: The Barstewards Enquiry @BarstewardsThe · 4h The industry has worked alongside with the commission, they welcomed afford
item.description: The Barstewards Enquiry @BarstewardsThe · 4h The industry has worked alongside with the commission, they welcomed affordability checks, did they not Dan? Did they not make punters stand outside their houses holding ID etc to get a withdrawal? Was that the gambling commission that enforced that? Cita Daniel Waugh @DanielWaugh6 · 4h En respuesta a @BarstewardsThe Excuse me? Perhaps you think it is ok for public bodies to fund activism, mislead policymakers and act against the laws they are required to uphold. If you don’t consider these things problematic, please tell me what the “real problem” is and why it excuses any of the above. 1 1 531

item.title: BarstewardsThe: The Barstewards Enquiry @BarstewardsThe · 5h In the last 5 years I have been affordability checked 4 times on an exchang
item.description: The Barstewards Enquiry @BarstewardsThe · 5h In the last 5 years I have been affordability checked 4 times on an exchange account that is 7 figures in profit. Stop the gaslighting of the real problem and who you do work for, the gambling commission is full of idiots. Cita Daniel Waugh @DanielWaugh6 · 5h En respuesta a @VaughanLewis1 @NevinTruesdale y @GamRegGB In the last five years, the Gambling Commission has repeatedly misrepresented statistics and research; funded activism against licensees, promoted anti-gambling opinions & speech codes and suppressed evidence on the basis of convenience. 2 1 742

The Barstewards Enquiry reposteó The Barstewards Enquiry @BarstewardsThe · 5h En respuesta a @19Rich_
@Stephenh61 y @nickluck It's not him though is it, he thought he was doing good, the books have approved it and got rid of sharps etc, withheld winnings from punters, you name it, slots turnover risen, racing turnover dropped, but he's to blame yes? 1 1 3 528

The Barstewards Enquiry reposteó James Noyes @jranoyes · 5h En respuesta a @YorkshireFlint y @BarstewardsThe Can we take a breath here for a moment and try to keep things civil?
It is true that Richard was an advocate of affordability checks from early on. See here: https:// racingpost.com/news/sky-bet-c hief-richard-flint-backs-culture-of-limits-for-online-betting-aFYPR1L1iFA0/ … And it is true that he encouraged me to focus on spend limits as an idea rather than stake Mostrar más racingpost.com Industry chiefs say gambling curbs need to strike right balance 1 1 2 340

Lydia Hislop reposteó Daniel Waugh @DanielWaugh6 · 5h En respuesta a @VaughanLewis1
@NevinTruesdale y @GamRegGB In the last five years, the Gambling Commission has repeatedly misrepresented statistics and research; funded activism against licensees, promoted anti-gambling opinions & speech codes and suppressed evidence on the basis of convenience. 1 3 7 1 mil

item.title: BarstewardsThe: The Barstewards Enquiry @BarstewardsThe · 6h You orchestrated the checks, James Noyes did not devise them you did, your
item.description: The Barstewards Enquiry @BarstewardsThe · 6h You orchestrated the checks, James Noyes did not devise them you did, your proposals, was this not true Richard, maybe expand on this? Cita Richard Flint @YorkshireFlint · 6h Respondiendo a @BarstewardsThe @david_murphy698 y 2 más What on earth are you talking about? 2 1 1 mil

item.title: RacingTV: Racing TV @RacingTV · 6h There is plenty to enjoy on our YouTube channel! Join over 210,000 other subscribers by clickin
item.description: Racing TV @RacingTV · 6h There is plenty to enjoy on our YouTube channel! Join over 210,000 other subscribers by clicking below De youtube.com 1 2 mil
